signatureSummary: Suspicious Web Flow Pattern matched as a possible Web Attack pattern.
signaturePlainExplanation: Suspicious web traffic was detected on an HTTP/HTTPS port. The flow used TCP, targeted a web service port, had relatively large packet sizes, and completed within a short time window. This pattern may indicate automated or abnormal web attack behaviour.
matchedConditionsReadable: Protocol is TCP
Destination port is 80 or 443
Mean packet length is at least 300 bytes
Forward packet mean length is at least 250 bytes
Flow duration is at most 5,000,000 microseconds

signatureTechnicalDetails.rationale: Web attack traffic commonly uses HTTP or HTTPS destination ports. Larger packet length means can indicate request-heavy or payload-heavy web flows. This flow-level heuristic cannot inspect URLs, request parameters, SQL strings, or XSS payloads.
matchedConditions: Protocol is TCP
Destination port is 80 or 443
Mean packet length is at least 300 bytes
Forward packet mean length is at least 250 bytes
Flow duration is at most 5,000,000 microseconds

signatureEvidence: Matched Suspicious Web Flow Pattern as predicted Web Attack using observable flow conditions: protocol=TCP, destinationPort {"oneOf":[80,443]}, packetLengthMean {"min":300}, fwdPacketLengthMean {"min":250}, flowDuration {"max":5000000}. Rule status: prototype-heuristic. Rationale: Web attack traffic commonly uses HTTP or HTTPS destination ports.

signatureSummary: SSH Brute Force Flow Pattern matched as a possible Brute Force pattern.
signaturePlainExplanation: Suspicious SSH login-style traffic was detected. The flow targeted port 22 and showed repeated packets within a short time window, which may indicate brute-force authentication attempts.
matchedConditionsReadable: Protocol is TCP
Destination port is 22
Flow packets per second is at least 10
Total forward packets is at least 10
Flow duration is at most 5,000,000 microseconds

signatureTechnicalDetails.rationale: SSH control traffic commonly uses TCP port 22. SSH brute-force attempts often create repeated short TCP flows against SSH port 22. This is a flow-level heuristic and cannot inspect SSH authentication results.
matchedConditions: Protocol is TCP
Destination port is 22
Flow packets per second is at least 10
Total forward packets is at least 10
Flow duration is at most 5,000,000 microseconds

signatureEvidence: Matched SSH Brute Force Flow Pattern as predicted Brute Force using observable flow conditions: protocol=TCP, destinationPort=22, flowPacketsPerSecond {"min":10}, totalFwdPackets {"min":10}, flowDuration {"max":5000000}. Rule status: prototype-heuristic. Rationale: SSH control traffic commonly uses TCP port 22.
